message:
perf(vdbe): promote DecrJumpZero into try_execute_hot_opcode hot-path

Fourth opcode-promotion lever on bd-1dp9.6.2, following AddImm
(64d8ce4d), Copy (20b05a95), and SCopy (bd1a49bc). `Opcode::DecrJumpZero`
is the canonical LIMIT counter: emitted 19× in codegen, fires once per
emitted result row in every LIMIT-bounded query. Body is a pair of
integer comparisons + `set_reg_int` + a conditional jump — small enough
for the dispatch-shortening to be a meaningful fraction of per-op cost.

Mirrors the existing main-match arm verbatim. Adds a parallel
`vdbe_pipeline_execute_decrjumpzero` microbench (counter seeded so the
decrement branch is the taken path, isolating dispatch cost from a
branch-prediction shift) and a `test_decr_jump_zero_via_hot_path` unit
test pinning the three-way state machine (counter>1 / counter==1 /
counter==0) to the hot-path arm.

Before/after on the new bench (criterion, p<0.05):

  vdbe_pipeline_execute_decrjumpzero/256   3.0561 us   ->  2.8920 us  (+5.0% thrpt, p=0.00)
  vdbe_pipeline_execute_decrjumpzero/1024  11.947 us   ->  11.290 us  (+6.5% thrpt, p=0.00)

Smaller win than earlier promotions (AddImm +9.9%, Copy +23.4%, SCopy
+38.6%) because DecrJumpZero's body carries two register writes and a
conditional jump, so dispatch is a smaller fraction of total per-op
cost. Still statistically significant and worth shipping: real LIMIT
queries fire this opcode on every result row.

Bench: `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cc2_vdbe_perf \
  cargo bench -p fsqlite-vdbe --bench pipeline_stages -- \
  vdbe_pipeline_execute_decrjumpzero --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 4`

`test_decr_jump_zero_via_hot_path` + existing `test_if_not_zero_decrements_and_jumps`
pass. Clippy (lib + tests + benches) clean via rch.

Bead: bd-1dp9.6.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
